commit.message:
Align records due-soon summary with active filters

Review pass fix: updates VehicleDetail records summary so due-soon counts reflect selected scope and date filters, preventing mismatched dashboard numbers.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add trip merge tools and time filters

Adds time-based filtering for fuel logs and vehicle records, improves trip bookmark proximity matching and bookmark creation workflow, introduces trip merge API and UI actions, and includes the pending VehicleRecord migration-order fix.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Make startup checkpoints consumable as explicit operator signals (#85)

Add a standalone startup checkpoint classifier that turns captured
nr-softmodem logs into a machine-readable categorized stream. Reuse
that direct-marker catalog inside the startup-trace report so the
standalone classifier and lifecycle trace do not drift apart.

Constraint: Acceptance requires machine-readable explicit categories
Constraint: Existing startup-trace derived and inferred behavior must stay intact
Rejected: Keep matcher rules only inside startup-trace | not a standalone operator surface
Rejected: Create a second independent CLI parser | higher drift risk
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep direct log-marker patterns in the classifier catalog and have other startup-log reports reuse it
Tested: mix test test/nr_softmodem_startup_checkpoint_classifier_test.exs test/nr_softmodem_startup_trace_test.exs test/cli_test.exs
Tested: mix lint
Tested: mix test
Tested: mix run -e '... runtime startup-checkpoints ...'
Tested: mix verify
Not-tested: real OAI captures with timestamped cross-stream interleaving
Related: YON-277

Co-authored-by: Developer <developer@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(hooks): harden plan-review issue binding and hook IO (#4044) (#4064)

* fix(hooks): replace branch-name digit scan with explicit ISSUE_NUMBER in subagent-stop.sh (#4044)

Two independent bugs in the plan-reviewer label gate both stemmed from
the same root: deriving the issue number by scanning the worktree branch
name for digits.

Bug: plan-reviewers run in generic slots named worktree-agent-<8hexchars>.
The old `grep -oE '[0-9]+' | head -1` scan extracted whatever decimal
digits followed the leading hex letter (e.g., 'a071b609' -> '71'),
labeling random merged PRs instead of the reviewed issue. The same scan
on branch 'a8fbba9a' extracted '8', which matched the issue body's
description of a 'hardcoded #8'.

Fix: remove the branch-name scan entirely. The hook now reads
ISSUE_NUMBER from the environment variable (explicit, highest priority)
or from the issue_number field in the stdin JSON payload. If neither
is a valid positive integer, the hook exits 3 (fail loud) rather than
silently mislabeling an unrelated issue or PR. The exit code 3 is
distinct from exit 2 (label-missing gate) for clear diagnostics.

Non-plan-reviewer agents are unaffected: the gate is guarded by
AGENT_TYPE matching 'plan-reviewer'.

Add 7 regression tests to .ci/scripts/test-hooks.sh covering:
- no ISSUE_NUMBER and hex-only branch -> exit 3
- ISSUE_NUMBER=0 -> exit 3
- ISSUE_NUMBER=abc -> exit 3
- builder agent (non-plan-reviewer) -> exit 0 (gate skipped)
- JSONL metrics still written when gate fails
- valid ISSUE_NUMBER env var -> does not exit 3
- issue_number JSON payload field (fallback path) -> does not exit 3

* fix(hooks): harden plan-review issue binding and hook IO (#4044)

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Validate no-TX probe prerequisites before hardware contact (#82)

The new validator derives its contract from HardwareProbePlanner so
the no-TX/read-only rules stay in one place. It adds a read-only CLI
surface that reports explicit preconditions, current environment
resolution for uhd_usrp_probe and OAI_GNB_SDR_DEVICE_ARGS, and
machine-readable failures without writing runtime history.

Constraint: Validator must stay read-only and avoid runtime history side effects
Constraint: Existing hardware probe planner remains the source of truth for no-TX rules
Rejected: Add a second standalone ruleset | would drift from the hardware.probe contract
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep environment checks derived from HardwareProbePlanner; do not duplicate probe safety rules in the CLI layer
Tested: mix test test/hardware_probe_environment_validator_test.exs test/cli_test.exs
Tested: mix lint
Tested: mix test
Tested: mix verify
Not-tested: Live host execution with a real uhd_usrp_probe binary

Co-authored-by: Developer <developer@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Ground parity reports in captured OAI control evidence (#75)

The parity report now accepts captured OAI control data from either an
inline map or a JSON file path, normalizes it against the known
lifecycle mapping, and makes fallback fields explicit when a capture is
partial. CLI and unit coverage now prove the captured-file path instead
of only the documented fallback path.

Constraint: No new dependencies allowed for JSON decoding
Rejected: Add a JSON library dependency | OTP already provides :json.decode/1
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep captured-vs-fallback evidence explicit; do not silently treat documented defaults as captured control data
Tested: mix test test/nr_softmodem_parity_evidence_report_test.exs test/cli_test.exs
Tested: mix verify
Tested: mix lint
Tested: mix test
Not-tested: real startup-trace capture artifact produced by a live OAI control run

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: support Project AI conversation deletion and retention (#659)

* feat(chat): add conversation deletion and retention

Summary:
- add Project AI conversation deletion across runtime, workspace, and
  conversation-scoped persistence with dirty-workspace safeguards
- persist project-level Project AI retention settings and run periodic
  retention cleanup with activity/audit events
- expose delete and retention controls in the web UI with refreshed tests

Rationale:
- make runtime close distinct from conversation deletion so users can
  explicitly reclaim workspaces and clean up stale history
- keep retention semantics typed at the domain boundary while skipping
  active, interrupted, and dirty conversations automatically
- surface lifecycle controls in both backend and frontend so cleanup is
  safe, observable, and understandable

Tests:
- PATH=$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$PATH OPENASE_PGTEST_SHARED_ROOT=$HOME/.cache/openase/pgtest go test ./internal/chat ./internal/app ./internal/repo/catalog ./internal/repo/chatconversation ./internal/domain/catalog
- PATH=$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$PATH OPENASE_PGTEST_SHARED_ROOT=$HOME/.cache/openase/pgtest go test ./internal/httpapi -run TestWriteProjectConversationErrorMappings
- PATH=$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$PATH OPENASE_PGTEST_SHARED_ROOT=$HOME/.cache/openase/pgtest go test ./internal/httpapi -run TestProjectConversationDeleteRouteDeletesConversationWhenAuthDisabled
- PATH=$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$PATH OPENASE_PGTEST_SHARED_ROOT=$HOME/.cache/openase/pgtest go test ./internal/httpapi -run TestParseAgentProjectPatchRequestSupportsFullProjectSurface
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel-actions.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-history-popover.test.ts src/lib/features/settings/components/general-settings.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* refactor(project-ai): extract panel and settings helpers

Summary:
- extract Project AI panel delete and interrupt flows into dedicated
  helpers and move conversation restore orchestration behind a focused
  restore helper
- split Project settings retention and archive UI into dedicated
  components and keep the mobile-safe settings surface intact
- add domain coverage for Project AI retention parsing and apply the
  lint-safe workspace resolution guard

Rationale:
- keep the new ASE-153 behavior easier to maintain without leaving the
  panel and settings components to absorb every workflow detail
- preserve CI-aligned mobile coverage when the local default Playwright
  port is occupied by another workspace
- satisfy the domain coverage and lint gates required before preview
  deploy

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH PLAYWRIGHT_WEB_PORT=4174 PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:4174 .codex/skills/push/scripts/openase_ci_gate.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* feat(admin): refresh auth governance layouts and defaults

Summary:
- Reworked the instance admin page into hash-synced sections for
  overview, sessions, and user directory flows.
- Refreshed auth governance UI blocks with denser list/detail layouts,
  collapsible audit timelines, and severity dot styling.
- Switched project AI retention from a checkbox row to a switch-led
  settings panel and updated related component tests.
- Updated InstanceAuthConfig defaults so session_ttl and
  session_idle_ttl now default to 0s.

Rationale:
- Improves scanability for instance admins managing session and user
  governance tasks on smaller and larger screens.
- Aligns default auth session behavior with non-expiring defaults until
  explicit TTL policies are configured.

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.0/bin:$PATH \
  .codex/skills/push/scripts/openase_ci_gate.sh (fails: max-lines in
  web/src/lib/features/settings/components/
  security-settings-user-directory-detail.svelte)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(settings): split user directory panels and docs

Summary:
- Extracted the user directory list and recent audit sections into

commit.message:
Make OAI control runs repeatable without live execution (#71)

Add a reducer-backed `oai.control_run` harness that emits the
`nr-softmodem` launch argv, observation commands, explicit side-effect
scope, and host-lifecycle references. The surface stays data-only and
reuses the existing boot planner so operators get a stable control-group
path without mutating the runtime launcher.

Constraint: Control-run output had to stay machine-readable and bounded
Constraint: Repo verification gate is `mix verify`
Rejected: Extend RuntimeLauncher with live process management | would
  add effects and widen scope beyond the issue
Rejected: Emit opaque shell snippets only | weaker auditing and
  side-effect clarity
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: moderate
Directive: Keep control-run launch mappings explicit; do not mark new
  split/transport combinations launch-ready without a verified default
  OAI config path
Tested: mix test test/nr_softmodem_control_run_harness_planner_test.exs
  test/catalog_test.exs test/planner_coverage_report_test.exs
  test/planner_metadata_lint_report_test.exs
  test/oai_gnb_elixir_test.exs test/cli_test.exs
Tested: mix verify
Not-tested: Live `nr-softmodem` process execution against the external
  OAI checkout
Related: YON-260

Co-authored-by: Developer <developer@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: lock notification event coverage and wiring (#657)

* fix(notification): align event contract coverage

Summary:
- unify notification rule catalog, runtime topic wiring, and explicit
  unsupported exclusions behind one shared contract
- emit ticket completed/cancelled, retry scheduled, and budget
  exhausted events from real product entry points and stop faking PR
  updates as pr.opened
- extend backend/frontend/docs coverage for the supported channels and
  notification event matrix

Rationale:
- the notification system had drift between declared rule events,
  actual emitters, engine wiring, and tests, which made it easy to add
  half-wired events
- centralizing the contract lets catalog, engine, emitters, and tests
  fail together when coverage regresses instead of silently drifting
- keeping pr.merged explicitly unsupported avoids shipping a rule for an
  event that still lacks a reliable canonical emitter

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/domain/notification ./internal/notification ./internal/orchestrator ./internal/httpapi
- cd web && PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
- cd web && PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH CI=1 pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/features/settings/notification-event-catalog.test.ts --reporter=verbose

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* test(chat): reduce mux keepalive flake

Summary:
- relax the mux keepalive stream test timeout so the initial HTTP
  handshake still succeeds when the full httpapi package runs under load
- keep the 5ms keepalive interval and repeated-run coverage so the test
  still verifies periodic SSE keepalives

Rationale:
- syncing this branch to the latest origin/main introduced a flaky
  timeout in the new chat mux keepalive test during package-wide runs
- stabilizing the test keeps workflow-aligned validation meaningful
  instead of blocking unrelated product work on ambient package load

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/httpapi -run TestProjectConversationMuxStreamRouteStreamsPeriodicKeepalives -count=3
- PATH=$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/domain/notification ./internal/notification ./internal/orchestrator ./internal/httpapi

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* style(web): format notification event catalog

Summary:
- apply Prettier formatting to the notification event catalog file
- keep the machine disconnect template variable block CI-clean

Rationale:
- the notification contract branch already passes the product changes, but
  frontend CI still fails on this file's formatting
- landing the branch requires the settings catalog to match the repo's
  enforced Prettier output exactly

Tests:
- in progress: `.codex/skills/push/scripts/openase_ci_gate.sh`

commit.message:
feat: add turn-level stop for Project AI conversations (#651)

* feat(chat): add turn-level stop for project ai

Summary:
- add a dedicated project conversation interrupt-turn API, CLI command,
  authorization rule, and OpenAPI surface for stopping only the active
  Project AI turn
- preserve reusable runtime anchors while marking turns and runs as
  interrupted, broadcasting interrupted/session events, and keeping mux
  delivery reliable
- update the Project AI controller, stream handling, and panel UI to show
  Stop during streaming, preserve partial output, finalize cleanly, and
  return to an idle reusable state with focused regression coverage

Rationale:
- closing the whole runtime was too coarse for the Stop UX because it tore
  down reusable conversation state instead of expressing a turn-only stop
- reusing the interrupted terminal path keeps backend and frontend stop
  semantics non-erroring while preserving already streamed assistant output
- adding dedicated API and parser coverage reduces the risk of mux or
  reconnect paths dropping interrupted terminal events

Tests:
- PATH=$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/chat ./internal/httpapi ./internal/cli
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm -C web exec vitest run src/lib/api/chat.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-stream.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-controller.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel-session.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel-actions.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-controller-actions.test.ts
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm -C web exec svelte-check --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore(openapi): sync interrupt-turn api artifacts

Summary:
- regenerate `api/openapi.json` for the project conversation interrupt-turn
  endpoint
- refresh `web/src/lib/api/generated/openapi.d.ts` so frontend types match
  the checked-in OpenAPI contract

Rationale:
- the CI openapi check requires generated API artifacts to stay in sync with
  the handwritten server route and schema updates
- committing the regenerated outputs keeps local and GitHub validation aligned

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH .codex/skills/push/scripts/openase_ci_gate.sh
  (failed before this commit at `make openapi-check-ci`, which generated
  these files)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* test(chat): split stop regression specs for lint limits

Summary:
- move interrupted stream assertions into a dedicated chat stream test file
- move the Project AI Stop panel flow into its own panel stop test file
- keep the original session and stream test files under the repo lint line
  limit without dropping stop-turn coverage

Rationale:
- the CI web lint gate enforces a 300 line cap per file
- splitting the new stop coverage keeps the regression tests readable while
  preserving the acceptance-path assertions added for ASE-145

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm -C web exec vitest run src/lib/api/chat.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-stream.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-stream-interrupted.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-controller.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel-session.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel-stop.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel-actions.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-controller-actions.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* test(chat): split provider status panel coverage

Summary:
- move the Claude and Codex provider-status panel assertions into a dedicated
  Project AI panel provider status test file
- shrink `project-conversation-panel-session.test.ts` back under the repo
  max-lines limit while keeping the live transcript coverage in place

Rationale:
- after merging the latest `origin/main`, the session panel test exceeded the
  enforced 300 line limit
- splitting the provider-status assertions preserves coverage and restores web
  lint compatibility for the files touched by this feature

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm -C web exec vitest run src/lib/api/chat.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-stream.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-stream-interrupted.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-controller.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel-session.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel-provider-status.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel-stop.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel-actions.test.ts src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-controller-actions.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(chat): cancel codex stop timeout context

Summary:
- inline the Codex interrupt-turn timeout context creation where it is used
- always defer the returned cancel function when a stop timeout is added

Rationale:
- satisfy the incremental gosec check that flagged the helper-returned
  cancel function as not being consumed
- keep stop requests bounded without changing Project AI turn-stop
  behavior

Tests:
- PATH=$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test ./internal/chat ./internal/httpapi ./internal/cli
- PATH=$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH make lint

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(chat): complete Project AI turn stop recovery

Summary:
- clear completed interrupted turns from active-turn lookups so follow-up
  prompts can start after a user stop
- drop stale Codex thread anchors on stop, retry the persisted stop marker
  on entry conflicts, and keep the interrupted turn reusable from the same
  conversation
- move the Project AI Stop control into the composer action slot and add
  regression coverage for the backend and frontend stop flows

Rationale:
- stopping a single reply should not leave the conversation locked behind
  PROJECT_CONVERSATION_TURN_ALREADY_ACTIVE or a dead Codex resume anchor
- real live stop testing exposed a race between the stop path and concurrent
  transcript writes, so the stop marker needs a small conflict retry
- putting Stop in the primary composer slot matches the intended UX and
  avoids conflating it with focused ticket-agent interrupts

Tests:
- PATH=$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test
  ./internal/repo/chatconversation -run
  'TestCreateTurnWithUserEntryAllowsNewTurnAfterCompletedInterruptedTurn'
- PATH=$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH go test
  ./internal/chat -run
  'TestProjectConversation(InterruptTurnRetriesEntryConflict|StartTurnAllowsNextTurnAfterUserStop|InterruptTurnStopsActiveRunAndBroadcastsReadySession)'
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm -C web
  exec vitest run src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-controller.test.ts
  src/lib/features/chat/project-conversation-panel-stop.test.ts
- live HTTP validation against local all-in-one on 127.0.0.1:19999:
  start turn -> interrupt-turn 202 -> interrupted + ready stream events ->
  next turn 202

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(web): stabilize CI after main sync

Summary:
- add budget waivers for the existing oversized Project AI and security
  settings files that remain in this branch diff after syncing with main
- remove the unused variable introduced by the merged access settings test
- update the skills editor Playwright test to edit the CodeMirror-backed
  skill body instead of a removed textarea selector

Rationale:
- the required branch sync to origin/main surfaced unrelated CI failures
  that blocked the Preview Deploy handoff even though the stop-flow work
  itself was complete
- these follow-up fixes keep the branch aligned with the current editor UI
  and the repo's file-budget policy without changing product behavior

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm -C web
  exec eslint src/lib/features/settings/components/access-settings.test.ts
  --quiet
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH corepack pnpm -C web
  exec playwright test tests/e2e/skills-editor.spec.ts --reporter=line
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PWD/.tooling/go/bin:$HOME/.local/go1.26.1/bin:$PATH \
  OPENASE_PGTEST_SHARED_ROOT=$HOME/.cache/openase/pgtest \
  OPENASE_GO_TEST_PROGRESS_MODE=json \
  .codex/skills/push/scripts/openase_ci_gate.sh

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

Expose the nr-softmodem host lifecycle as a first-class runtime contract (#64)

The runtime launcher now exports the staged host phases documented in
`docs/NR_SOFTMODEM_LIFECYCLE.md` so launch output reflects the top-level
softmodem lifecycle instead of only the internal launch reducer steps.

A dedicated host lifecycle helper keeps the phase catalog and transition
trace machine-readable, while the launcher reducer still owns side effects
through the existing boot-plan, session-allocation, and session
materialization events. The published runtime contract now separates the
launcher reducer transition table from the host lifecycle transition table
so callers do not lose the effect-aware reducer model.

Constraint: YON-255 is the scaffold lane; deeper per-phase semantics land in follow-up parity tickets
Rejected: Replacing the launcher reducer contract with host transitions only | mixed host transitions with launcher effects under one surface
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep `transition_table` and `effect_inventory` aligned to the same reducer contract; publish host lifecycle metadata on separate fields
Tested: mix test test/runtime_launcher_test.exs test/cli_test.exs
Tested: mix verify
Not-tested: Shutdown events and teardown behavior remain catalog-only in this lane
Related: YON-255

Co-authored-by: Developer <developer@example.com>
